title: Confidence Scaling - Assessment Practice
learning_objective: Learn to use confidence scaling to assess readiness and explore what would increase or decrease confidence about making changes
scenario_context: Scaling questions use a numeric scale (typically 0-10) to assess confidence, importance, or readiness. They provide a concrete way to measure subjective experiences and explore what would move someone up or down the scale. This core skill applies universally to customer and colleague contexts.

persona_config.name: Riley
persona_config.background: Is considering making changes but feels uncertain about their ability to succeed. Has some motivation but also significant doubts.

node_1.persona_text: I'm thinking about making this change, but honestly, I'm not sure if I can actually do it. Part of me thinks I can, but another part worries I'll fail.

choice_points.option_text: Of course you can do it! You just need to believe in yourself.

feedback.immediate: You've offered reassurance before understanding their actual confidence level. Scaling would help you assess where they really are.
feedback.learning_note: Scaling questions assess before we encourage or advise.

choice_points.option_text: On a scale from 0 to 10, where 0 means not at all confident and 10 means completely confident, how confident are you that you could make this change?

feedback.immediate: Perfect scaling question! This gives you a concrete measure of their confidence and opens exploration.
feedback.learning_note: Scaling questions provide a measurable way to assess subjective states.

choice_points.option_text: Why do you think you'd fail?

feedback.immediate: You've focused on their doubt rather than exploring their overall confidence. Scaling would be more balanced.
feedback.learning_note: Scaling questions assess both confidence and doubt together.

node_2b.persona_text: I'd say about a 6. I think I can do it, but I'm not completely sure.

choice_points.option_text: A 6 isn't bad, but we need to get you to a 10 before you start.

feedback.immediate: You've set an unrealistic standard. People can and do make changes with confidence less than 10.

choice_points.option_text: A 6 is pretty good. You're more confident than not. What helps you be at a 6 rather than lower?

feedback.immediate: Excellent! You've validated their confidence while exploring what contributes to it. This builds on strengths.
feedback.learning_note: Exploring what maintains higher confidence identifies resources and strengths.

choice_points.option_text: What would need to happen for you to move from a 6 to a higher number?
choice_points.preview_hint: This explores what would increase confidence

feedback.immediate: Great scaling follow-up! This helps them identify what would increase their confidence.
feedback.learning_note: Scale exploration questions help identify specific steps or resources needed.

node_3b.persona_text: Well, I've made changes before, so I know I can do it. And I have some support from people who believe in me.

choice_points.option_text: So you have past experience with making changes and people who support you - those are real strengths that help you feel confident.

feedback.immediate: Excellent affirmation! You've reflected their strengths and validated the resources they've identified.
feedback.learning_note: Affirmations after scaling exploration reinforce confidence and resources.

node_3c.persona_text: I think having a clear plan would help. And maybe breaking it down into smaller steps so it doesn't feel so overwhelming.

choice_points.option_text: So having a clear plan and breaking things into smaller steps would increase your confidence.

feedback.immediate: Perfect reflection! You've captured their own ideas about what would help. This is their solution, not yours.
feedback.learning_note: Scaling exploration helps people identify their own solutions.

node_2a.persona_text: I suppose. Thanks for the encouragement.

node_2c.persona_text: It's not that I think I'd fail, I just have some concerns.

node_3a.persona_text: That feels like too much pressure. Maybe I'm not ready.

node_end_excellent.persona_text: This has been really helpful. I feel more confident now that I can see my strengths.

node_end_good.persona_text: Thanks for helping me think through this. I have a better sense of what I need.
